zh.Over Peover, Cheshire, England, United K: 英國英格蘭爾奧維 奧福
zh.Great Budworth, , Cheshire, England: 英國爾格亞特 布德沃斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth De Leicester於1318年出生在英國爾塔波時，她的父親Nicholas Leicester19歲，母親Mary de Mobberley17歲。她於大約1343年與William De Mainwaring IV of chester在英國爾米德列維其結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1406年在英國爾米德列維其享年88歲，埋葬在英國爾馬頓。

zh-hans.Over Peover, Cheshire, England, United K: 英国英格兰爾奧維 奧福
zh-hans.Great Budworth, , Cheshire, England: 英国爾格亞特 布德沃斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth De Leicester于1318年出生在英国爾塔波时，她的父亲Nicholas Leicester19岁，母亲Mary de Mobberley17岁。她于大约1343年与William De Mainwaring IV of chester在英国爾米德列維其结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和6个女儿的父母。她去世于1406年在英国爾米德列維其享年88岁，埋葬在英国爾馬頓。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Leicester
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Leicester (Leics).

variants.name: Lisseter
meanings: English:  see  Leicester  .

variants.name: Lester
meanings: English: habitational name from Leicester, which is recorded as Ligeraceastre in the 10th century. The placename derives from an Old English folk name Legore ‘the dwellers by the river Legor (a lost river name)’ + Old English ceaster ‘city, Roman fortification’.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Lestre in Normandy.
English and Scottish: variant of   Lister  .

variants.name: Lessiter
meanings: English:  see  Leicester  .

variants.name: Lassiter
meanings: English: habitational name from the city of Leicester (see   Lester  ). This name has been long established in North America, and occurs in a wide variety of forms.

variants.name: Lasseter
meanings: English: variant of Lassiter (see Lester ).

variants.name: Lestor
meanings: English:  see  Leicester  .

variants.name: Leister
meanings: English: variant of   Lester  .
German: occupational name for a maker of lasts or a cobbler, from Middle High German leist + the agent suffix -er.

variants.name: Leycester
meanings: English:  see  Leicester  .

variants.name: Laister
meanings: English:  see  Leicester  .

variants.name: Lassetter
meanings: English: variant of Lassiter (see Lester ).

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth De Leicester was born in 1318, in Tabley, Cheshire, England, her father, Nicholas Leicester, was 19 and her mother, Mary de Mobberley, was 17. She married William De Mainwaring IV of chester about 1343, in Middlewich, Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 6 daughters. She died in 1406, in Middlewich, Cheshire, England, at the age of 88, and was buried in Marton, Cheshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth De Leicester est née en 1318, à Tabley, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Nicholas Leicester, avait 19 ans et sa mère, Mary de Mobberley, avait 17 ans. Elle a épousé William De Mainwaring IV of chester environ 1343, à Middlewich, Cheshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée en 1406, à Middlewich, Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 88 ans, et a été enterrée à Marton, Cheshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth De Leicester 1318 in Tabley, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Nicholas Leicester 19 und ihre Mutter Mary de Mobberley 17. Sie hat um 1343 in Middlewich, Cheshire, England William De Mainwaring IV of chester geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb 1406 im Alter von 88 Jahren in Middlewich, Cheshire, England und wurde in Marton, Cheshire, England begraben.

Lei sposato William De Mainwaring IV of chester circa 1343, nel Middlewich, Cheshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 6 figlie. Morì nel 1406, nel Middlewich, Cheshire, England, all'età di 88 anni, e fu sepolta a Marton, Cheshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth De Leicesterが1318年にイギリスチェシュアイアタブリーで生まれた時，父Nicholas Leicesterは19歳，母Mary de Mobberleyは17歳であった。約1343年にイギリスチェシュアイアミッドルイチでWilliam De Mainwaring IV of chesterと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼女は1406年にイギリスチェシュアイアミッドルイチで88歳で死去し，イギリスチェシュアイアマルトンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth De Leicester(이)가 1318년 영국 체실르 타블리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Nicholas Leicester(은)는 19세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mary de Mobberley(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 대략 1343년 영국 체실르 미들루이치에서 William De Mainwaring IV of chester(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1406년 영국 체실르 미들루이치에서 88세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 체실르 마턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth De Leicester nasceu em 1318, em Tabley, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Nicholas Leicester, tinha 19 anos e sua mãe, Mary de Mobberley, tinha 17 anos. Ela casou-se com William De Mainwaring IV of chester aproximadamente 1343, em Middlewich, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 6 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1406, em Middlewich, Cheshire, Inglaterra, com 88 anos, e foi sepultada em Marton, Cheshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth De Leicester родилась в 1318 г. в местности табли, чешир, Англия, её отцу по имени Nicholas Leicester было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary de Mobberley, 17. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William De Mainwaring IV of chester около 1343 г. в местности миддльвич, чешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Она умерла в 1406 г. в местности миддльвич, чешир, Англия в возрасте 88 лет и была похоронена в местности мартон, чешир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth De Leicester nació en 1318, en Tabley, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Nicholas Leicester, tenía 19 años y su mamá, Mary de Mobberley, tenía 17 años. Se casó con William De Mainwaring IV of chester aproximadamente 1343, en Middlewich, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió en 1406, en Middlewich, Cheshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 88 años, y fue sepultada en Marton, Cheshire, Inglaterra.
